questions.promptTex: \text{A finishes in } 10 \text{ days, B in } 15 \text{ days. Together time (days)?}
hints: Or LCM(10, 15) = 30; A = 3, B = 2; together = 5; time = 30/5 = 6.

steps.text: **LCM trick:** LCM(10, 15) = 30. Set total work = 30 units.
**Rates:** A = $30/10 = 3$ units/day, B = $30/15 = 2$ units/day. Together: $5$ units/day.

questions.promptTex: \text{A in } 8 \text{ days, B in } 12 \text{ days. Together time (days)?}
\text{A, B together do work in } 12 \text{ days. A alone in } 18 \text{ days. B alone (days)?}
questions.explanation: B's rate = $1/12 - 1/18 = 1/36$. So B alone takes 36 days.

questions.promptTex: \text{Pipe A fills a tank in } 5 \text{ h. Pipe B empties it in } 10 \text{ h. Both open: time to fill (h)?}
hints: LCM(5, 10) = 10. A = +2 units/h, B = −1 unit/h. Net = +1 unit/h.

questions.promptTex: \text{A is twice as efficient as B. B alone takes } 30 \text{ days. Time together (days)?}
hints: Together: LCM(30, 15) = 30; A = 2, B = 1, together = 3. Time = 10 days.
questions.explanation: A alone: 15 days. Together: $1/15 + 1/30 = 3/30 = 1/10$. Time: 10 days.

steps.text: **'Twice as efficient'** means A does the same work in half the time as B. B alone in 30 days, so A alone in 15 days.
**LCM trick:** LCM(15, 30) = 30 units of total work.
A's rate: $30/15 = 2$ units/day. B's rate: $30/30 = 1$ unit/day. Together: $3$ units/day.

questions.promptTex: \text{A, B, C finish in } 6, 8, 12 \text{ days. Together (days)?}
\text{10 men finish a job in } 18 \text{ days. How many days will } 15 \text{ men take?}
questions.explanation: Total work: 180 person-days. With 15 men: $180/15 = 12$ days.

questions.promptTex: \text{A finishes in 12 days, B in 16. They work together for } 4 \text{ days, then A leaves.}\\\text{Total elapsed days from start until B finishes (round to 2 decimals)?}
hints: LCM(12, 16) = 48 work-units. A's rate = 4, B's rate = 3, together = 7.
In 4 days together: 28 units done. Remaining: 20 units. B alone: 20/3 ≈ 6.67 more days.
questions.explanation: Use LCM as total work: 48 units. Together they do 7 units/day, so 4 days together = 28 units. Remaining 20 units take B (rate 3/day) another $20/3 \approx 6.67$ days. Total elapsed time: $4 + 20/3 = 32/3 \approx 10.67$ days.

steps.text: **LCM trick.** LCM(12, 16) = 48 work-units. A's rate = $48/12 = 4$ units/day, B's = $48/16 = 3$ units/day. Together = 7 units/day.
**In 4 days together.** $7 \times 4 = 28$ units done. Remaining: $48 - 28 = 20$ units.
**B alone finishes the remaining 20 units at rate 3:**
**Total elapsed (4 days together + B's solo time).** $4 + 20/3 = 12/3 + 20/3 = 32/3 \approx 10.67$ days.

questions.promptTex: \text{Two pipes fill in } 4 \text{ and } 6 \text{ h. A leak empties full tank in } 24 \text{ h.} \\ \text{Time to fill with all three open (h)?}
hints: LCM trick. Set tank capacity = LCM(4, 6, 24) = 24 units.
Per-hour rates: pipe 1 = $24/4 = 6$ (fills), pipe 2 = $24/6 = 4$ (fills), leak = $24/24 = 1$ (drains). Net = $6 + 4 - 1 = 9$ units/h.
questions.explanation: Set tank capacity = LCM(4, 6, 24) = 24 units. Filling rates: $+6$ and $+4$. Leak rate: $-1$. Net: $+9$ units/h. Time to fill 24 units: $24/9 \approx 2.67$ h.

questions.promptTex: \text{4 men or 6 women finish a job in } 12 \text{ days. How long for } 2 \text{ men and } 3 \text{ women?}
hints: 4 men's rate = 6 women's rate (same job in 12 days). So 1 man = 1.5 women, or 2 men = 3 women.
2 men + 3 women = 6 women's equivalent = same as 4 men's rate. Same 12 days.
questions.explanation: 2 men + 3 women = 3 women + 3 women = 6 women equivalent (since 2 men = 3 women). Equivalent to original crew → 12 days.

steps.text: **Convert men to women-equivalents.** 4 men and 6 women do the same job in 12 days, so $4M = 6W$, i.e. $1M = 1.5W$ (or $2M = 3W$).
**This equals the original 6 women's effort** → same 12 days.

questions.promptTex: \text{Combined } A \text{ and } B \text{ in } 4 \text{ days. } A \text{ alone in } 6 \text{ days.} \\ \text{B alone (days)?}
\text{A finishes in } 20 \text{ days. A works } 4 \text{ days, then B joins. Together they finish remaining in } 8 \text{ days. B alone (days)?}
hints: A's rate = $1/20$ per day. After 4 days A has done $4/20 = 1/5$, leaving $4/5$.
A+B finish $4/5$ in 8 days → A+B rate $= (4/5)/8 = 1/10$ per day.
B's rate = $1/10 - 1/20 = 1/20$. So B alone = 20 days.
questions.explanation: A's rate = 1/20. After 4 days, 1/5 done; 4/5 remains. A+B finish 4/5 in 8 days, so A+B rate = 1/10 per day. B's rate = 1/10 - 1/20 = 1/20. B alone = 20 days.

steps.text: **Work done by A alone in 4 days:** $4 \times 1/20 = 1/5$. Remaining: $1 - 1/5 = 4/5$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Pipe fills a tank in } 6 \text{ h. Outlet empties full tank in } 8 \text{ h. Both open: time to fill (h)?}
hints: LCM(6, 8) = 24. In rate +4, out rate −3, net +1.

steps.text: Inlet rate (+): $24/6 = 4$ units/h. Outlet rate (−): $24/8 = 3$ units/h.

questions.promptTex: \text{If } 8 \text{ workers can do a job in } 10 \text{ days, how many workers needed to finish in } 4 \text{ days?}
questions.explanation: Total: 80 person-days. To finish in 4 days: $80/4 = 20$ workers.

questions.promptTex: \text{Wages for a job: } A \text{ alone gets } \$6000. \, A \text{ does it in } 10 \text{ days, } B \text{ in } 15 \text{ days.} \\ \text{They work together; what's B's share of wages (\$)?}
hints: Wages should be in ratio of rates: A : B = 1/10 : 1/15 = 3 : 2.
B's share = 2/5 of total. Total wage (if A alone gets \$6000 for the whole job) is \$6000. B's share = \$2400.
questions.explanation: Ratio of work done: A:B = 1/10 : 1/15 = 3:2. B's share = 2/5 of \$6000 = \$2400.

steps.text: **Wages divide in the ratio of the work each does** (= ratio of their rates).
**Multiply both by LCM(10, 15) = 30** to clear fractions:
